zh.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen是Rutger Willemsen van Essen和Maijken Philipsen的兒子，於1536年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德普爾圖加阿爾。他於大約1555年與Haesgen van Driel結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。他去世於1624年12月26日在荷蘭-拉藍德萊登享年88歲，埋葬在荷蘭-拉藍德萊登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen是Rutger Willemsen van Essen和Maijken Philipsen的儿子，于1536年出生在荷兰-拉藍德普爾圖加阿爾。他于大约1555年与Haesgen van Driel结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。他去世于1624年12月26日在荷兰-拉藍德萊登享年88岁，埋葬在荷兰-拉藍德萊登。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Essen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called Essen, for example in the Ruhr.

variants.name: Esson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans.
Scottish (Aberdeenshire): from a shortened and altered form of the Gaelic personal name Adamh (see Adam ). This surname was sometimes confused with 2 below.
English and Scottish: variant of Easton , although in some instances the Scottish surname may reflect the reverse process, in which the name in 1 above has acquired an intrusive -t-.

variants.name: Essman
meanings: Americanized form of German Essmann: habitational name, a variant of Essen , with the suffix man ‘man’ or nickname for a big eater, from the agent derivative of Middle High German ezzen ‘to eat’.

nameMeanings.name: Willem
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: William of Orange was recognized as viceroy of Holland, Friesland and Utrecht.
The siege of Leiden between the rebels and the Spanish. 1574 The dikes were opened allowing a Dutch fleet to sail over the flood waters and rescue Leiden.
The Dutch declared their independence from Spain.
The United Provinces declare their independence from Spain.
Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn was born in Leiden, the Netherlands.
"English navigator Henry Hudson, under contract to the United East India Company, sails from Amsterdam and ""discovers""Manhattan Island and the future site of New York."

en.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen was born in 1536, in Poortugaal, South Holland, Netherlands as the son of Rutger Willemsen van Essen and Maijken Philipsen. He married Haesgen van Driel about 1555. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. He died on 26 December 1624, in Leiden, South Holland, Netherlands, at the age of 88, and was buried in Leiden, South Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen est né en 1536, à Poortugaal, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, fils de Rutger Willemsen van Essen et Maijken Philipsen. Il a épousé Haesgen van Driel environ 1555. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Il est décédé le 26 décembre 1624, à Leiden, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 88 ans, et a été enterré à Leiden, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen wurde 1536 in Poortugaal, Südholland, Niederlande, als Sohn von Rutger Willemsen van Essen und Maijken Philipsen geboren. Er hat um 1555 Haesgen van Driel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. Er starb am 26. Dezember 1624 im Alter von 88 Jahren in Leiden, Südholland, Niederlande und wurde in Leiden, Südholland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen è nato il nel 1536 nel Poortugaal, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi come figlio di Rutger Willemsen van Essen e Maijken Philipsen. Lui sposato Haesgen van Driel circa 1555. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie. Morì il 26 dicembre 1624, nel Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 88 anni, e fu sepolto a Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Willem van Essenは1536年にオランダズイド-hオランドポオルトゥガアルでRutger Willemsen van EssenとMaijken Philipsenの息子として生まれた。約1555年にHaesgen van Drielと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。彼は1624年12月26日にオランダズイド-hオランドレイデンで88歳で死去し，オランダズイド-hオランドレイデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen(은)는 1536년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 포어투가앨에서 Rutger Willemsen van Essen(와)과 Maijken Philipsen의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1555년 Haesgen van Driel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1624년 12월 26일 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 레이든에서 88세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 레이든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen nasceu em 1536, em Poortugaal, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos filho de Rutger Willemsen van Essen e Maijken Philipsen. Ele casou-se com Haesgen van Driel aproximadamente 1555. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 26 de dezembro de 1624, em Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, com 88 anos, e foi sepultado em Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen родился в 1536 г. в местности пуртугааль, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды. Его родителей звали Rutger Willemsen van Essen и Maijken Philipsen. Он женился на женщине по имени Haesgen van Driel около 1555 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Он умер 26 декабря 1624 г. в местности лиден, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды в возрасте 88 лет и был похоронен в местности лиден, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Willem van Essen nació en 1536, en Poortugaal, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos como hijo de Rutger Willemsen van Essen y Maijken Philipsen. Se casó con Haesgen van Driel aproximadamente 1555. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. Murió el 26 de diciembre de 1624, en Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, a la edad de 88 años, y fue sepultado en Leiden, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos.
